topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating persistence. Liam watched Maya stride confidently toward the hiring manager’s desk, a bright, almost effortless smile playing on her lips. His stomach tightened into a hard knot. He had spent weeks polishing his application, refining his interview answers, pouring every ounce of his ambition into this opportunity. Now, seeing her secure the position he desperately craved, a bitter taste flooded his mouth. He forced a casual clearing of his throat when the manager finally turned to him, his practiced composure feeling brittle. "I'm very enthusiastic about this role," Liam managed, the words sounding thin and reedy even to his own ears. He noticed Maya catching his eye across the room, a brief, knowing flicker that made his jaw clench.

[story 2]

"You absolutely have to go for it, Alex," Chloe urged, clapping him heartily on the back. Alex managed a tight, strained smile, his fingers gripping the edge of the coffee mug so hard his knuckles were white. He watched as Chloe’s enthusiasm radiated outwards, a warm, undeniable glow that seemed to draw the attention of everyone around them. When the notification finally arrived—Maya had been selected—Alex felt a strange hollowness settle in his chest. He walked to the window, staring out at the bustling street, the noise of the city suddenly overwhelming. He picked up his phone, his thumb hovering over the contact for Maya, a fleeting impulse to ask about the experience, quickly stifled by a wave of sharp, unwelcome frustration.

[story 3]

Sarah meticulously reviewed the job description one last time, the details blurring slightly as she reread the requirements. She imagined herself in the role, the prestige, the validation. Then, she saw the email confirmation: Maya. A slow, cold wave washed over Sarah. She felt a sudden, intense heat rising in her cheeks, a flush she couldn't control. When her colleague, Ben, asked if she was okay, Sarah offered a laugh that sounded far too bright. "Just thrilled for Maya," she said, the words tasting like ash. She focused intently on straightening a stray piece of paper on her desk, her movements jerky, desperate to appear completely unbothered while the internal churning intensified.
